name: Trim PTX 64x64 Microkernel Register Footprint Back To 3-CTA Class

summary.name: Trim PTX 64x64 Microkernel Register Footprint Back To 3-CTA Class
summary.hypothesis: The round-10 wait-group collapse restored the PTX anchor and fixed the barrier and short_scoreboard stalls, but it caused registers/thread to jump 168 -> 202 because the restored microkernel keeps 16 f32 accumulator fragments plus live A/B fragments across both 64x64 row pairs. By reordering ptx_wmma_accumulate_row_pair_64x64_ptx_microkernel so the two row pairs are interleaved column-by-column (Right-Left-Right-Left across RowPairBase = 0 and 2) instead of sequentially, and by reducing the simultaneous live PtxWmmaBf16Fragment count, we should be able to hold registers/thread below ~180 and restore launch__occupancy_limit_registers = 3. That directly attacks the dominant occupancy_latency_hiding_issue (severity 43.22) without touching barrier wins.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Register-pressure-bound occupancy: 202 regs/thread caps SM residency at 2 CTAs, starving the long_scoreboard / mio_throttle latency hiding budget at 16.58% active warps.
summary.risk: Moderate. The microkernel ordering is performance-sensitive but fully preserves the WMMA math; correctness risk is low and register pressure can be verified with ncu launch__registers_per_thread on the first compile.
ranking_notes: This is the single best upside/risk tradeoff: the dominant bottleneck class (occupancy_latency_hiding_issue, severity 43.22) is directly caused by the 34-register delta produced last round.
If registers fall back to the 168 class, occupancy_limit_registers returns to 3 and we regain most of the latency hiding lost this round, while keeping the barrier stall wins of round 10.
Maps onto the human guidance family Register Reuse (Right-Left-Right-Left warp tile) almost literally, so the direction is also audit-aligned with state/human_guidance.md.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if launch__registers_per_thread does not fall below 190 after the interleave rewrite, or if the rewrite regresses barrier above 8% or runtime above 25.5 ms.
expected_local_changes: Rework ptx_wmma_accumulate_row_pair_64x64_ptx_microkernel so the two row pairs are interleaved column-by-column instead of both sequentially materialized, shrinking the live A-fragment window.
Confirm nvcc does not inflate fragment state by adding --maxrregcount guard only if necessary, avoiding spills to local memory.
Leave wait_group_0 / wait_group_1 ordering from round 10 untouched so recovered barrier and short_scoreboard gains are preserved.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
actual_code_regions: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: rewritten ptx_wmma_accumulate_tile_set_64x64_ptx_microkernel body to drive 4 column steps of interleaved Right-Left row-pair calls (~lines 904-922)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: ptx_wmma_accumulate_row_pair_64x64_ptx_microkernel left intact (lines 742-818) as __forceinline__ and is DCE'd since nothing calls it now
